 {"id":"bd-8l6b","title":"Create Test Worker Configuration (workers_test.toml)","description":"## Purpose
Create a test-only worker configuration that points to real, available SSH-accessible worker machines for true e2e testing.

## Requirements
1. `tests/e2e/workers_test.toml` file with at least 1 real worker
2. Worker must have: passwordless SSH, Rust nightly, rsync, zstd
3. Validate worker is actually reachable via SSH
4. Document worker requirements for CI setup

## Configuration Format
```toml
# tests/e2e/workers_test.toml

[workers.css]
hostname = "css.example.com"
user = "builder"
port = 22
identity_file = "~/.ssh/id_ed25519"
remote_work_dir = "/tmp/rch_test"

# Optional: multiple workers for load distribution
[workers.gpu2]
hostname = "gpu2.example.com"
user = "builder"
remote_work_dir = "/data/rch_test"

[settings]
default_timeout_seconds = 300
ssh_connection_timeout = 10
rsync_compression = "zstd"
```

## Implementation Notes
- Can use the same workers as production (css, gpu2, etc)
- Should include health check to skip tests if workers unavailable
- Consider env var `RCH_E2E_WORKERS_CONFIG` to override

## Unit Tests for Config Validation

```rust
// tests/true_e2e/tests/config_tests.rs

#[test]
fn test_config_parses_valid_toml() {
    let config_str = r#"
        [workers.test]
        hostname = "test.example.com"
        user = "builder"
    "#;
    let config: WorkerConfig = toml::from_str(config_str).unwrap();
    assert_eq!(config.workers.len(), 1);
    assert_eq!(config.workers["test"].hostname, "test.example.com");
}

#[test]
fn test_config_rejects_missing_hostname() {
    let config_str = r#"
        [workers.test]
        user = "builder"
    "#;
    let result: Result<WorkerConfig, _> = toml::from_str(config_str);
    assert!(result.is_err());
}

#[test]
fn test_config_default_port() {
    let config_str = r#"
        [workers.test]
        hostname = "test.example.com"
        user = "builder"
    "#;
    let config: WorkerConfig = toml::from_str(config_str).unwrap();
    assert_eq!(config.workers["test"].port.unwrap_or(22), 22);
}

#[test]
fn test_config_expands_home_in_identity_file() {
    let config_str = r#"
        [workers.test]
        hostname = "test.example.com"
        user = "builder"
        identity_file = "~/.ssh/id_ed25519"
    "#;
    let config: WorkerConfig = toml::from_str(config_str).unwrap();
    let expanded = expand_path(&config.workers["test"].identity_file.as_ref().unwrap());
    assert!(!expanded.to_string_lossy().contains("~"));
}

#[test]
fn test_config_from_env_override() {
    env::set_var("RCH_E2E_WORKERS_CONFIG", "/custom/path.toml");
    let path = get_config_path();
    assert_eq!(path, PathBuf::from("/custom/path.toml"));
    env::remove_var("RCH_E2E_WORKERS_CONFIG");
}

#[test]
fn test_config_missing_file_returns_error() {
    let result = load_worker_config("/nonexistent/workers_test.toml");
    assert!(result.is_err());
    assert!(result.unwrap_err().to_string().contains("not found"));
}

#[test]
fn test_config_empty_workers_is_valid_but_useless() {
    let config_str = r#"
        [settings]
        default_timeout_seconds = 300
    "#;
    let config: WorkerConfig = toml::from_str(config_str).unwrap();
    assert!(config.workers.is_empty());
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Config file exists and is valid TOML
- [ ] At least one worker can be reached via SSH
- [ ] Worker has required toolchain installed
- [ ] Unit tests for config validation pass
- [ ] Config handles missing/invalid gracefully with clear errors","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:21:55.353755779Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T10:48:37.417413247Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T10:48:37.417350309Z","close_reason":"Created test worker configuration infrastructure: workers_test.toml template and rch-common/src/e2e/test_workers.rs module with 26 passing tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-8l6b","depends_on_id":"bd-3saj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:11:38Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-8o08","title":"Create CompilationProgress for build status visualization","description":"Create CompilationProgress in rch-common/src/ui/progress/compile.rs for build tracking:
- Crate compilation progress (X/Y crates)
- Current crate name and version
- Elapsed time with rate (crates/minute)
- Build phase indicator (Compiling, Linking, Running tests)
- Memory usage indicator for large builds

Technical requirements:
- Parse cargo output for crate count extraction
- Handle both release and debug build indicators
- Show warning count accumulator
- Support incremental build detection (fewer crates)
- Display linker phase separately (often the longest part)
- Integrate with worker resource monitoring for memory display

Example during compilation:
⚙ Building on worker1  [████████████████░░░░░░░░░░░░] 67%
   82/122 crates │ 45.2s elapsed │ 1.8 crates/sec
   Compiling serde_json v1.0.108
   ⚠ 3 warnings accumulated

Example for linking phase:
⚙ Linking target/release/myapp...  ◐
   122/122 crates compiled │ 67.3s │ Linking (12.4s)

Example on completion:
✓ Build complete: 122 crates in 79.7s (1.5 crates/sec, 3 warnings)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:10:20.906760815Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T21:23:33.708140348Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T21:23:33.708093049Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-8o08","depends_on_id":"bd-37o8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:11:38Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-8o08","depends_on_id":"bd-39mp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:11:38Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-9eml","title":"Unit Tests: Fleet Management Module (No Mocks)","description":"# Unit Tests: Fleet Management Module (No Mocks)

## Target Files

All in `rch/src/fleet/`:
- plan.rs - Fleet planning logic
- executor.rs - Fleet execution engine  
- preflight.rs - Pre-flight checks
- dry_run.rs - Dry-run simulation
- history.rs - History tracking
- rollback.rs - Rollback functionality
- audit.rs - Audit trails

## Test Strategy

### Test Design (No Mocks)

1. **Use TempDir**: All file operations use real temp directories
2. **Use Localhost**: Command execution tests run real commands locally
3. **Use Real Files**: Cargo.toml, src/main.rs are actual files
4. **Test Real Behavior**: Verify actual output, not mocked responses

### Required Tests Per File

| File | Tests |
|------|-------|
| plan.rs | plan_single_worker, plan_multi_priority, plan_empty_error |
| executor.rs | execute_real_command, capture_stderr, respect_timeout |
| preflight.rs | check_real_paths, detect_missing_cargo, validate_toolchain |
| dry_run.rs | dry_run_logs_only, dry_run_no_side_effects |
| history.rs | persist_to_file, load_from_file, append_entries |
| rollback.rs | create_checkpoint, restore_checkpoint, cleanup_temp |
| audit.rs | log_event, persist_audit_trail, query_by_date |

### Test Template

```rust
#[test]
fn test_plan_single_worker() {
    // Structured logging
    tracing::info!("TEST START: plan_single_worker");
    
    // Real temp directory
    let tmp = TempDir::new().unwrap();
    
    // Real operation
    let plan = FleetPlan::new(&workers, tmp.path());
    
    // Real assertion
    assert_eq!(plan.workers().len(), 1);
    
    tracing::info!("TEST PASS: plan_single_worker");
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] 3+ tests per module (21 total minimum)
- [ ] All tests use real filesystem operations
- [ ] All tests have TEST START/PASS logging
- [ ] No mock usage (except for external SSH)
- [ ] `cargo test fleet` passes","notes":"Verified comprehensive test coverage:

**Tests per module (requirement: 3+):**
- audit: 24 tests ✅
- dry_run: 31 tests ✅
- executor: 8 tests ✅
- history: 14 tests ✅
- plan: 31 tests ✅
- preflight: 16 tests ✅
- rollback: 7 tests ✅

**Total: 131 fleet tests (requirement: 21 minimum) ✅**

All tests pass. No mocks detected in test code.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WildSpring","created_at":"2026-01-25T22:57:51.268727446Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T03:15:34.862252991Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T03:15:34.862181978Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9eml","depends_on_id":"bd-1aim","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:11:38Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-9ka6","title":"WS4: Smart Multi-Command Classification","description":"PROBLEM: rch-common/src/patterns.rs line ~547 blanket-rejects any command containing "&&" via contains_unquoted_str(). This means legitimate compilation chains like "cargo fmt && cargo build" or "cd project && make" are rejected and forced to run locally, even though they contain compilable work.

WHY IT MATTERS: AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex) commonly chain commands with &&. Blanket rejection means a significant fraction of compilation work is never offloaded, defeating the purpose of RCH.

SOLUTION: Split on && and || delimiters, classify each sub-command independently, and offload if ANY sub-command is a compilation command. The result should be "compilable" if at least one segment classifies as compilation.

CONSIDERATIONS:
  - Must handle quoting: "echo '&&' && cargo build" — the first && is inside quotes
  - Must handle pipes within segments: "cargo build 2>&1 | tee log" is one segment
  - Performance: splitting adds ~1-2μs but saves the entire remote compilation benefit
  - Edge case: "rm -rf target && cargo build" — the rm is destructive but cargo build is compilable. The hook's job is classification, not safety — if any part compiles, offload the whole thing (the worker runs the original command as-is).

FILES: rch-common/src/patterns.rs (Tier 1 rejection logic, new split_commands helper)
RISK: High — changes core classification semantics. Needs comprehensive test coverage for quoting edge cases.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:36:49.247323586Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T17:36:49.257058770Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9ka6","depends_on_id":"bd-2mmt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:36:49.257009759Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-9ka6","title":"WS4: Smart Multi-Command Classification","description":"PROBLEM: rch-common/src/patterns.rs line ~547 blanket-rejects any command containing "&&" via contains_unquoted_str(). This means legitimate compilation chains like "cargo fmt && cargo build" or "cd project && make" are rejected and forced to run locally, even though they contain compilable work.

WHY IT MATTERS: AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex) commonly chain commands with &&. Blanket rejection means a significant fraction of compilation work is never offloaded, defeating the purpose of RCH.

SOLUTION: Split on && and || delimiters, classify each sub-command independently, and offload if ANY sub-command is a compilation command. The result should be "compilable" if at least one segment classifies as compilation.

CONSIDERATIONS:
  - Must handle quoting: "echo '&&' && cargo build" — the first && is inside quotes
  - Must handle pipes within segments: "cargo build 2>&1 | tee log" is one segment
  - Performance: splitting adds ~1-2μs but saves the entire remote compilation benefit
  - Edge case: "rm -rf target && cargo build" — the rm is destructive but cargo build is compilable. The hook's job is classification, not safety — if any part compiles, offload the whole thing (the worker runs the original command as-is).

FILES: rch-common/src/patterns.rs (Tier 1 rejection logic, new split_commands helper)
RISK: High — changes core classification semantics. Needs comprehensive test coverage for quoting edge cases.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:36:49.247323586Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T17:39:36.543397855Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9ka6","depends_on_id":"bd-2mmt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:36:49.257009759Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":82,"issue_id":"bd-9ka6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"BACKGROUND: AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor) frequently emit chained commands:
  cargo fmt && cargo build && cargo test
  cd /project && make -j$(nproc)
  export CC=clang && cmake -B build && cmake --build build

The current Tier 1 rejection of && means NONE of these are offloaded. This is arguably the single highest-impact fix in this audit — it directly increases the fraction of compilable work that gets offloaded.

DESIGN DECISION: We classify sub-commands independently but execute the ORIGINAL command string on the worker. The hook does not rewrite commands — it only decides local vs. remote. This preserves the fail-open design: if our splitting is imperfect, the worst case is local execution (safe).

PERFORMANCE IMPACT: split_commands adds ~1-2μs to classification. But the benefit is offloading a 30-60 second compilation to a remote worker. The ROI is >1000x.

RISK MITIGATION: Comprehensive test suite (WS4.3) covers quoting edge cases. The splitter must handle single quotes, double quotes, backticks, and escaped characters correctly. A bug here could cause misclassification.","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:39:36Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-9ygg","title":"Test: Project rsync Transfer to Workers","description":"## Purpose
Test that project files are correctly synced to workers via rsync with proper exclusions and compression.

## MANDATORY Logging
This test MUST use TestLogger for all phases:

```json
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_rsync_initial","phase":"sync","msg":"Starting rsync","data":{"source":"fixtures/hello_world","dest":"css:/tmp/rch_test","worker":"css"}}
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_rsync_initial","phase":"sync_complete","msg":"Rsync completed","data":{"files_transferred":15,"bytes_sent":12345,"duration_ms":1234}}
{"ts":"...","level":"DEBUG","test":"test_rsync_exclusion","phase":"verify","msg":"Exclusion check","data":{"pattern":"target/","excluded":true}}
```

## Test Cases

### Basic Sync
1. Initial sync:
   - Sync hello_world fixture to worker
   - Verify: all source files present on worker
   - Verify: correct permissions
   - Log: files transferred, bytes, duration

2. Incremental sync:
   - Modify one file, sync again
   - Verify: only changed file transferred
   - Verify: rsync reports minimal transfer
   - Log: incremental vs full comparison

3. Sync with deletions:
   - Delete local file, sync
   - Verify: file removed on worker (or not, based on config)
   - Log: delete behavior

### Exclusion Patterns
4. target/ excluded:
   - Local target/ exists
   - Verify: not synced to worker
   - Log: exclusion verified

5. .git/objects excluded:
   - Verify: .git/objects not synced
   - But: .gitignore IS synced (needed for build)
   - Log: .git handling

6. node_modules excluded:
   - Verify: not synced to worker
   - Log: exclusion verified

7. Build artifacts excluded:
   - .rlib, .rmeta, .o files not synced
   - Log: artifact exclusion

### Compression
8. zstd compression:
   - Verify rsync uses compression
   - Verify: transfer size reduced
   - Log: compression ratio

### Edge Cases
9. Large files:
   - File > 100MB
   - Verify: transfer completes
   - Log: large file handling

10. Many files:
    - Project with > 10000 files
    - Verify: sync completes within timeout
    - Log: file count, sync time

11. Symlinks:
    - Project contains symlinks
    - Verify: handled correctly (follow or preserve)
    - Log: symlink handling mode

12. Special characters:
    - Files with spaces, unicode names
    - Verify: correctly synced
    - Log: special char handling

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All files sync correctly to real worker
- [ ] Exclusions work (target/, .git/objects/, etc)
- [ ] Incremental sync is efficient
- [ ] Large projects sync within timeout
- [ ] No data corruption
- [ ] ALL phases logged with structured JSON","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:24:32.533703690Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T00:29:54.017622702Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T00:29:54.017375016Z","close_reason":"Merged into bd-1f2v (Project Sync Tests).","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9ygg","depends_on_id":"bd-1f2v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:11:38Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-9ygg","depends_on_id":"bd-2a7t","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:11:38Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-9ygg","depends_on_id":"bd-2bid","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:11:38Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-anzk","title":"Add --help-json and --capabilities flags for machine-readable discovery","description":"Add --help-json to emit help as JSON and --capabilities to list all features. Enables agents to discover functionality programmatically.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T05:43:00.796529776Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T23:32:45.322492964Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T23:32:45.322411392Z","close_reason":"Implemented --help-json + --capabilities (bdb54e6) and fixed early-flag handling before clap parse (58905c0).","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-aruz","title":"WS2.3: Optimize cache key normalization","description":"rch/src/cache.rs line ~181 allocates a new String for cache key normalization on every lookup.

Options:
- Pre-compute normalized keys at insertion time
- Use a hash directly instead of normalizing to String
- Use Cow<str> to avoid allocation when no normalization needed

Lower priority than the pattern constants fix.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:37:26.457593688Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T17:37:26.466990622Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-aruz","depends_on_id":"bd-3uo1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:37:26.466966247Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
